author: potuz
content: Yeah just make sure your user accepts the trusted part though. It should be the sun only on builders the user really trusts

author: potuz
content: The pubkey they can have whole though

author: potuz
content: Again just make sure to specify that what is sent in the beacon API ahead of time does not necessarily need to be forwarded to the endpoint in the builder API ahead of time

author: potuz
content: If you use the exact same builder preferences that you will send in the jit call. I believe Prysm hasn't implemented this difference yet (cc <@363800010518822915> ) but it makes sense to have the builder preferences being sent in the first call to be different than the second one jit. And just forward verbatim the first one to the builders

author: potuz
content: We should probably change names to make this explicit: the first call is information for the builder to prepare the bid, the second call is information for the beacon to choose the bid. I hope we are on the same page here cause this has been a source of confusion all around

author: nflaig
content: well that's not how obol dvt (charon) works since the vcs only have a key share, so the pubkey is not the whole pubkey

author: nflaig
content: but if the pubkey is not being signed over, then it shouldn't be a big deal, they can reconstruct whole pubkey on the middleware before submitting to beacon node

author: potuz
content: Yeah that's what I'm saying that the full pubkey is something they *can* have and send to the beacon. Anyway the pubkey is not strictly necessary here. Dependent root+slot is enough

author: nflaig
content: calling the second one `BuilderPreferencesRequestV1` that we send jit is confusing imo

author: potuz
content:
Actually <@554799849288105986> from an implementation perspective I think it's easier for the ahead of time endpoint to pass in epoch N, the data of the dependent root of N, and a list of objects that contain the slot, preferences, etc 

So the beacon just sends the corresponding builders: I'm going to be calling at these slots with this dependent root and the builder is in charge of figuring out which ones are the proposers

author: terencechain
content:
Ahead of time, we send: `max_exec_payment + auth`
Just in time, we send: `urls, pubkeys, max_exec_payment, auth` (only auth is forwarded to the builder), we are missing `boost` and `min_bid` here, i will add it now

as for naming, i have them as `BuilderPreferencesRequest` and `BuilderRequestEntry`, you are right, they suck. The naming should sound more like "prepare the bid" vs "choose the bid"

do we need to pass `max_exec_payment` just in time? whats the use of that? that information should be cached ahead of time right? and builders cant take `max_exec_payment` just in time, that's not enough time to seal a block(?). This will simplify things a lot

author: potuz
content: I think this is fine. I don't think proposers want to expose boost nor min bid

author: potuz
content: As for the ahead vs jit vs both discussion, well, everyone will have a saying and we will never agree. All I'll say it's that the beacon needs it to decide the bid, so even if you don't forward it jit, it needs to be in the beacon API to produce block v4

author: terencechain
content:
Right i agree max trusted should be in JIT, but if we dont need that for AOT, this will significantly simplify any design. In fact, we dont need a seperate AOT call at all
The question is whether the builder is ok to ONLY take max trusted just-in-time
